regulator: IRDAI (Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India)

types.name: Term Insurance
types.definition: Pure protection plan. Pays the sum assured to the nominee only if the insured dies during the policy term. No maturity benefit if the insured survives.
types.best_for: Primary breadwinners who want maximum coverage at minimum cost.
key_features: Lowest premium among life insurance products
Sum assured can be ₹50 lakh to several crores
Policy terms: 10 to 40 years
Riders available: accidental death, critical illness, waiver of premium
types.example: A 30-year-old can get ₹1 crore cover for ~₹8,000-12,000 per year.
types.tax_benefit: Premium up to ₹1.5 lakh under Section 80C; death benefit tax-free under Section 10(10D)

types.name: Endowment Plan
types.definition: Combination of insurance and savings. Pays sum assured on death OR maturity — whichever is earlier.
types.best_for: Risk-averse investors wanting guaranteed returns with life cover.
key_features: Higher premium than term insurance
Guaranteed maturity benefit
Bonus (reversionary + terminal) declared annually by insurer
Surrender value available after 3 years
types.caution: Returns are typically 4-6% — lower than PPF or mutual funds. Primarily for protection, not wealth creation.

types.name: ULIP (Unit Linked Insurance Plan)
types.definition: Combines life insurance with market-linked investment. Premium is split between life cover and investment in equity/debt funds.
types.best_for: Long-term investors (10+ years) who want insurance + market-linked returns.
key_features: 5-year lock-in period (IRDAI mandate)
Fund switching allowed between equity, debt, balanced options
Charges: premium allocation, policy admin, fund management, mortality
After 5 years: partial withdrawals allowed
types.tax_benefit: Premium up to ₹2.5 lakh/year: tax-free maturity under Section 10(10D). Above ₹2.5 lakh premium: maturity taxable.
types.caution: High charges in initial years. Compare fund performance and total charges before buying.

types.name: Whole Life Insurance
types.definition: Provides life cover for the entire lifetime (typically till age 99 or 100). Premium paying term is limited (e.g. 20 years) but cover continues.
types.best_for: Estate planning, leaving legacy for dependents.

types.name: Individual Health Insurance
types.definition: Covers hospitalisation, surgery, ICU, daycare procedures for one individual.
key_features: Sum insured: ₹2 lakh to ₹2 crore
Cashless treatment at network hospitals
Pre and post-hospitalisation expenses covered
Waiting period: 30 days initial; 2-4 years for pre-existing diseases
No-claim bonus: 5-50% increase in sum insured for claim-free years
types.tax_benefit: Premium up to ₹25,000 for self/spouse/children under Section 80D; ₹50,000 for senior citizen parents.

types.name: Family Floater Health Insurance
types.definition: Single policy covering entire family (self, spouse, children). Sum insured is shared across all members.
types.best_for: Families where all members are young and healthy.
types.caution: If one member has a major claim, remaining sum insured reduces for others in that year.

types.name: Critical Illness Insurance
types.definition: Lump sum payout on diagnosis of covered critical illnesses (cancer, heart attack, stroke, kidney failure, etc.).
types.key_feature: Pays lump sum regardless of actual hospital bills — can be used for treatment, income replacement, or any purpose.

types.name: Motor Insurance
types.definition: Insurance for vehicles. Mandatory by law under Motor Vehicles Act.

types.Third Party: Mandatory by law. Covers damage/injury to third parties. Does NOT cover your own vehicle.
types.Comprehensive: Covers third-party liability + own damage + theft. Recommended for all vehicles.

key_features: IDV (Insured Declared Value): market value of vehicle — this is maximum claim amount
NCB (No Claim Bonus): discount up to 50% on own-damage premium for claim-free years
Zero Depreciation add-on: full claim without depreciation deduction — recommended for new cars

types.name: Home Insurance
types.definition: Covers structure and/or contents of home against fire, theft, flood, earthquake, and other perils.

types.Contents Insurance: Covers furniture, electronics, jewellery inside the home

types.name: Travel Insurance
types.definition: Short-term insurance for domestic or international travel.

key_concepts.Sum Assured: The guaranteed amount the insurer will pay on a claim or maturity. Choose based on income replacement need (typically 10-15x annual income for life insurance).
key_concepts.Premium: The amount you pay to keep the insurance policy active. Can be annual, half-yearly, quarterly, or monthly.
key_concepts.Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR): Percentage of claims settled by an insurer out of total claims received. Higher is better. IRDAI publishes this annually. Look for >95%.
key_concepts.Free Look Period: 15 days (30 days for distance marketing policies) to review a new policy. Full refund if cancelled within free look period.
key_concepts.Surrender Value: Amount you receive if you cancel a life insurance policy before maturity. Available only after paying premiums for minimum 3 years.
key_concepts.Grace Period: Extra time (15-30 days) to pay premium after due date without policy lapsing.
key_concepts.Rider: Add-on benefit attached to a base insurance policy for additional coverage (e.g. accidental death rider, critical illness rider).
key_concepts.Nominee: Person designated to receive the insurance payout upon the insured's death.
key_concepts.Pre-existing Disease (PED): Any illness/condition that existed before buying health insurance. Usually covered after a waiting period of 2-4 years.

irdai_regulations.know_your_customer: Full KYC mandatory for policies above ₹1 lakh annual premium.
irdai_regulations.claim_settlement_timeline: Insurers must settle claims within 30 days of receiving all documents. Delayed claims accrue interest.
irdai_regulations.grievance_redressal: Insurer must resolve complaints within 15 days. Escalate to Insurance Ombudsman if unresolved.
irdai_regulations.insurance_ombudsman: Free dispute resolution for claims up to ₹50 lakh. 17 ombudsman offices across India.
irdai_regulations.mis_selling_protection: Agents must explain policy terms clearly. Free look period protects against mis-selling.
irdai_regulations.bima_sugam: IRDAI's upcoming digital platform for buying, servicing, and claiming insurance online (one-stop marketplace).

how_to_choose: Life Insurance: Buy pure term for maximum coverage at minimum cost. Coverage = 10-15x annual income.
Health Insurance: Buy individual or family floater of at least ₹5-10 lakh. Top-up plan for additional cover.
Motor Insurance: Comprehensive + Zero Depreciation add-on for new vehicles.
Check Claim Settlement Ratio (>95%) and solvency ratio (>150%) of the insurer before buying.
disclaimer: Insurance is a subject matter of solicitation. Please read the policy document carefully before buying. Terms and conditions apply.
